title: Senior Procurement Specialist - EPC
description:
Your new company

Our client is seeking an EPC Procurement Specialist to strengthen their expanding EPC services team and drive excellence across key projects. Located at Jurong East with a hybrid working arrangement.

Your new role

Strategic Contracting & Tendering

  • Develop project-specific contracting strategies aligned with scope, risk, and market conditions from feasibility through execution.
  • Manage end-to-end tender processes, including pre-qualification, tender evaluations, tender boards, and contract awards for EPC-related scopes up to EUR 5 million.

Commercial & Contract Management

  • Lead negotiations to secure optimal commercial terms, manage variation orders, and ensure compliance with procurement governance and industry standards.
  • Administer procurement contracts, track performance, and ensure visibility, adherence, and effective utilisation.

Supplier & Contractor Management

  • Maintain and govern approved supplier lists; organise vendor assessments, site visits, and technical/commercial reviews.
  • Track contractor performance and ensure safety, quality, and delivery expectations are met.

Procure-to-Pay Execution

  • Oversee P2P workflows via ERP systems to ensure timely delivery of EPC materials, equipment, and services.

Project & Category Strategy Development

  • Conduct internal and external analysis (market trends, cost drivers, risk assessments) to shape category strategies that optimise cost, quality, and operational continuity.

Budgeting, Spend & Process Improvement

  • Execute annual procurement plans, analyse spend, identify opportunities for improvement, and align initiatives with project and operations stakeholders.
  • Review procurement processes and propose enhancements to improve efficiency, transparency, and control.

Performance Measurement

  • Track KPIs including savings, contractor performance, safety compliance, delivery reliability, and P2P cycle times; recommend actions to drive improvement.

What you'll need to succeed

  • Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Engineering, or related field.
  • Minimum 5 years’ experience in procurement, contract management, or supply management.
  • Strong commercial awareness and understanding of contract law.
  • Proven negotiation and vendor management skills.
  • Excellent communication, analytical, and stakeholder management abilities.
  • Proficiency in MS Office and ERP systems.

What you'll get in return

Ready to take on a strategic procurement role and make an impact? Apply now and start your journey toward leadership in procurement!

What you need to do now

If you're keen on pursuing this exciting opportunity, please send a copy of your CV (word document) to belinder.kaur@hays.com.sg (Registration ID No. R1545727)
EA Licence No.: 07C3924
Company's Registration number: 200609504D

At Hays, we believe the right job can transform a person's life and the right person can transform a business. We're passionate about connecting our candidates with the right job for them.
